consensus.default_position: Use structured primary-care assessment and optimise HRT/progestogen first in low-risk early bleeding, with clear triggers for urgent ultrasound and cancer-pathway escalation.
key_thresholds: Adjustment window: up to 6 months total if bleeding starts within 6 months of starting HRT or persists up to 3 months after regimen change (low risk)
Urgent TVUS (within 6 weeks): first bleed >6 months after starting HRT; persistent bleed >3 months post-change; heavy/prolonged bleeding; or additional risk factors
Endometrial thickness low-risk reassurance threshold: <=4 mm (continuous combined HRT), <=7 mm (sequential HRT) when fully visualised and uniform

summary: Primary care can manage selected early/low-risk bleeding with regimen optimisation
Use explicit imaging triggers and endometrial thickness cut-offs
